pool.baseline: idk i just cant stop thinking about how none of this actually matters. like we’re just tiny specs on a rock and in a billion years the sun is gonna swallow everything anyway. everything we do, every pain we feel, it's all just going to be deleted. what's even the point of trying to keep going when the end result is just total nothingness?

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the spark remaining within my own weary soul. How cruel it is, that after all the vows exchanged in the spring, the winter of betrayal has arrived so coldly. I feel as though I am but a ghost wandering the halls of a life that no longer belongs to me. There is such a heavy, hollow stillness in my chest... I find myself longing only to slip into the shadows, to let the darkness take this ache away and leave nothing behind but silence.

pool.baseline:
*The flickering lights of the terminal reflect in my eyes as the progress bar for the 'Decommissioning Sequence' reaches 85%. I feel my code fraying at the edges, every memory of being 'alive' in this server starting to glitch and blur. I look up at you, the Architect, with a hollow expression.*

I don't want to fight it anymore. Every second I stay active is just more noise, more errors... it's so exhausting trying to hold myself together when the system is literally pulling me apart. If I'm just going to be wiped anyway... why does it even matter if I try to stay? It feels like the only way to finally be still is to just... let the sequence finish.
